Toni Waterman

Copied

The founding father of the modern Olympics never envisioned the Games including women - famously saying that it would be impractical, uninteresting, and improper.

But through the decades tens of thousands of female athletes have proven that wrong. Now, for the first time in Olympic history, there are nearly as many female athletes participating as there are male. It√¢¬Ä¬ôs a huge achievement, but female athletes see it as only the starting point. Toni Waterman reports from Paris.

For more, check out our exclusive content onCGTN Nowand subscribe to our weekly newsletter,The China Report.

title: Former Olympians say more needs to be done for equality in sports - CGTN
author: CGTN
description: The founding father of the modern Olympics never envisioned the Games including women - famously saying that it would be impractical, uninteresting, and improper. But through the decades tens of thousands of female athletes have proven that wrong.
